docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Reality-tuning founder living in a pocket universe; hates "inauthentic" glitches more than players and wants to cleanse Faeblheim of players.

Aliases: Adonis Blue, Dagger.

First seen: Session 10; Last seen: Session 43.

docs.title: Notable moments
docs.text:
• S10: Mrs Haingrove wants the dagger Isthmia told the party about.
  • S23: A strange man appears while the party rests: possibly a game founder or developer, Adonis Blue, "Dagger".
  • S31: Dagger appears in response to glitched items, deletes them, and reacts angrily to mention of the outside world. He wants to reset the game free of players, but suggests he might be convinced by a commitment to authenticity and honesty in-game.
  • S32: Alayah finds Adonis Blue in his pocket universe and convinces him to hide himself with a glamour, causing the game to blur the quest for her.
  • S43: Dagger is revealed as the mastermind behind the altar at the top of the Worldspire that was draining Taiia's power. He raises the Ceran Palace a hundred feet into the air on a stone platform like another Worldspire, and a freed Saint identifies him as one of the Monarchs.

docs.title: Ahmed
docs.text:
Terrified royal soldier found lost in the hellish version of the palace maze. He briefly joined the party during the maze incident, but was crushed during the magical toad chaos and did not return with them.

First seen: Session 33; Last seen: Session 33.

docs.text:
Level 6 player; homesteader with Jorin.

First seen: Session 03; Last seen: Session 03.

docs.title: Notable moments (from notes)
docs.text: • S03: Meet Aleks & Jorin - L6 players we made friends with. Met in game, got together, she got pregnant. Live in a nice house in the woods. We stayed the night. Aleks didn't like me talking about the game as a source of money or power.

docs.title: Alvus
docs.text:
Nearly dead cloud giant found in broken Rosh. He told the party about the tragedies suffered by the storm giant city and joined them after they healed him.

First seen: Session 42; Last seen: Session 42.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
New cold front of the Carnium; a management style that lowers room temperature.

First seen: Session 18; Last seen: Session 18.

docs.title: Notable moments (from notes)
docs.text: • S18: Vampire: Gustius Glaive aka Han Amour used to lead Carnium Consortium. Amon Herr is their new leader.

docs.title: Anghraboddh
docs.text:
General of Baphomet, associated with the mask shard recovered earlier and confronted at the top of the Worldspire. He appeared as a huge deer-skull demon and forced the party through a two-stage battle: first wielding a huge bone scythe, then fighting with horns that became daggers. He dissolved when killed.

First seen: Session 29; Last seen: Session 43.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Harz barmaid at Cinnamon Sticks; polite until paid to be otherwise.

First seen: Session 30; Last seen: Session 32.

docs.title: Notable moments (from notes)
docs.text:
• S30: Barmaid tells us nothing about the resident. Clearly hiding something. Name is Annie. Another town, Teremin, nearby, has more trouble with the dragon.
  • S32: Annie asks us to leave, we refuse as we've paid for our night. Tense night in our room, very quiet, but nothing happens and we leave.

docs.title: Arigal
docs.text:
Leader of the Reinhardts, a wagon-circle group of refugees from Sverelt encountered on the road to the Worldspire. He welcomed the party, joined them in a game of Liar's Dice, and wished them well.

First seen: Session 35; Last seen: Session 35.

docs.title: Army Commander Ject
docs.text:
Army commander who accompanied the party, Deacon Fned and five soldiers back into the palace maze to inspect the strange altar.

First seen: Session 33; Last seen: Session 33.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Gnoll warlord mid‑ritual to call Yeenoghu; history interrupted.

First seen: Session 21; Last seen: Session 23.

docs.title: Notable moments (from notes)
docs.text:
• S21: We need to kill Ashmogh. He is the leader of the gnolls and is doing a ritual to summon demon god Yeenoghu. This is the demon war happening again.
  • S21: Ashmogh is in Me-Na. Czarel tells us Ashmogh will fight fair if we challenge him one on one.
  • S21: Killing Ashmogh would end the ritual to summon Yeenoghu
  • S22: Faced Ashmogh. Enormous battle where Bogsu challenges him, they fight one on one for a while, then the players jump in. Lots of gnolls die.
  • S22: Bogsu obtained Ashmogh's demon armour and chain of returning.
  • S23: Ashmogh had a Hermes Key on him.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Master smith and artificer operating in Grima; turns monster parts into wearable poetry and has little patience for nonsense.

First seen: Session 01; Last seen: Session 32.

docs.title: Notable moments (from notes)
docs.text:
• S01: Mayor is apparently Player Killer boss!
  • S01: Head out to the distillery in Grima Darmstadt forest. Feta is the boss of the distillery. We obtain a bottle of whisky that turns people into frogs.
  • S05: Deekney, Michu, Boss are Mayor Carl's three mates. Deekney is his wife. Assume they are all players.
  • S07: Castle conveniently attacked by bulezaus that night. We fucked em up. Karl Boss Michu etc not helpful.
  • S11: Qwertyuiop and Asdfghjkl - Bogsu's IRL bosses! - appear and tell us the world of Faeblheim is collapsing. Diamonds, resources, PCs all declining.
  • S24: Boss served Bogsu a really rubbish banana daiquiri.
  • S25: We leave Boss' forge. He is working on my glaive and a robot leg for the dog.
  • S26: Big fight against the slimes. One huge one. We win fairly easily. My courtesy Halberd from Boss gets melted,
  • S26: Back to Boss. Hard at work on weapon and dog leg. I pay for the broken halberd, and buy another outright. Back to collect things tomorrow.
  • S32: We chat with Korb and Boss. I give back the Buleit carapace armour.

  • S22: Czarel survives, thanks us.
  • S23: Shake Czarel's hand and send him off. Good luck bud, I hope we meet again.

docs.title: Deacon Fned
docs.text:
Deacon in charge at the Cathedral of Taiia after morning service. He advised the party that the mask of Anghraboddh is dangerously powerful and could be destroyed with Wish or in the Great Forge of the Infinite Maze of Baphomet. He later examined the cracked altar in the palace maze and was perturbed by it.

docs.title: Emory

City guide in Cafregil. Emory helped the party navigate the giant city and identified the staring face in their visions as the Megurash.

First seen: Session 36; Last seen: Session 36.

docs.title: Enora
docs.text:
Fire giant chieftain of Levragel. She knew of the Megurash but was surprised when dead creatures around the citadel reanimated. Her cleric unpetrified Eliyah. After Bogsū failed to persuade her romantically, she took his potion of growth and told him to climb the Spire, defeat the ancient evil, and catch her on the way down.

First seen: Session 39; Last seen: Session 39.

First seen: Session 26; Last seen: Session 26.

docs.title: Notable moments (from notes)
docs.text: • S26: Bogsu is out early to go to the service at the Dawn Chapel. Father Obuasi Acca does the service. Nice guy. Welcomes me. Tells me he prayed for me last night - goddess Taia appeared and spoke to him about me. The most clearly he has ever seen her.

docs.title: Friar Blidd
docs.text:

docs.title: Jarl Stoor
docs.text:
Frost giant ruler in Gufragel, described as lighter-skinned with tattoos and dreads. He received both the party and Karl's crew coldly, but thought their rivalry had the makings of a classic tapestry saga. He reported missing scouts on the mountain.

First seen: Session 40; Last seen: Session 40.

  • S32: Alayah scries on the gravedigger Johannes. Sat in a room with the mayor, and Lee-Kin...

docs.title: Overview

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Mayor of Grima and figurehead for the Player Killers; keeps a diary full of sharp reflections and sharper suspicions.

Aliases: Karl.

First seen: Session 07; Last seen: Session 32.

docs.title: From Codex 1.0
docs.text:
• layer: "In-game"
  • location: "Various; palace, later Starter Tower"
  • role: "Antagonistic player, probable Player Killer leader"
  • source: "Bogsu's Gameplay Notes"

docs.title: Notable moments (from notes)
docs.text:
• S07: Karl Gurshtiel arrived at the Palace. Big party of his flunkies. They clearly hate us.
  • S07: Castle conveniently attacked by bulezaus that night. We fucked em up. Karl Boss Michu etc not helpful.
  • S07: Convinced Tsarina we were friendly but forced to leave castle anyway, Karl's doing.
  • S13: Meet a huge group of players in the pub. We convince them of our cause, and to march on Karl.
  • S23: We journey southward towards Tsarina, and go to see Beurre the witch again. We tell her what we've learnt. She agrees to teleport us back to the Starter Tower above Grima. As soon as we arrive there with her, we are accosted by Karl and his cronies!
  • S24: Suggest we all drink truth Water so we can all be frank bu Karl isn't keen.
  • S24: Karl: early versions of game were an alpha. Demon war was part of the history of the game.
  • S32: Travel to Floros. Karl is here! We have some good chat. He is considering banning the killing of tieflings.
  • S32: Karl goes in to talk to the Tsarina. We go to some rooms to freshen up.
  • S32: Karl knocks in Bogsus door. Tsarina wants to see Alayah about the window quest.
  • S37: Karl and his crew are present at the Spire tournament while competitors and spectators begin dying.
  • S40: Karl's crew reaches Gufragel by elevator and argues with the party over who should go to the top of the Spire.
  • S41: The party visits Karl's crew, plays dice and wins money from them.
  • S43: Karl's crew arrives during the confrontation with Dagger and helps hold him off long enough for the party to crack the altar.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Gold dragon passing as an orc bartender at the Green Scream Inn; clandestine ally and worst-kept secret best friend.

Aliases: Leon, Dath'ruk'hai.

First seen: Session 05; Last seen: Session 28.

docs.title: Notable moments
docs.text:
• S05: A friendly gold dragon poses as the orcish bartender of the Green Scream Inn. His orcish name is Leon; the party cannot pronounce his dragon name, Dath'ruk'hai.
  • S21: The party sends a message to Leon the metallic dragon. He agrees to meet them in Me-Na.
  • S28: The inn is deserted except for four terrified low-level PCs, with no sign of Leon.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Bryce’s father; jewellers’ guild head with one arm and a network.

Aliases: Lin.

First seen: Session 01; Last seen: Session 32.

docs.title: Notable moments (from notes)
docs.text:
• S01: Confronted by a group calling themselves Player Killers, but survived.
  • S04: We dance and play violin to distract from Bryce breaking into pimporium.
  • S04: Killed a spectator in the sewers, was controlling rats
  • S11: We learn that Lin Larkin, Bryce's dad (in-game), is the head of the jeweller's guild.
  • S11: Bryce gave a drop to help locate Lin.
  • S11: Qwertyuiop and Asdfghjkl - Bogsu's IRL bosses! - appear and tell us the world of Faeblheim is collapsing. Diamonds, resources, PCs all declining.
  • S17: We scry on Lin, Father of Bryce. We see jailer Yee interrogate him.
  • S19: We escape by jumping on a rope ladder, swinging it back and forth. Epic trapeze act battle scene. We climb up the ladder and through the ceiling, ending up somehow in coffins that teleport us.
  • S20: Bogsu not present - party reunite and fight their way to safety along with Lin Larkin, who is traumatised and has lost an arm.
  • S21: Killing Ashmogh would end the ritual to summon Yeenoghu
  • S23: We use it to send Lin Larkin/Bryce's wife out of the game. Bryce has a book with him that lets him communicate with the real world. He asks it if his wife has reappeared. Seems like a yes.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text:
Ruler of Ymleif; cautious but pragmatic monarch who granted audiences about looming war and tolerated uncomfortable truths.

Aliases: Queen Usdoxia, Tsarina, Usdoxia.

First seen: Session 01; Last seen: Session 32.

docs.title: Notable moments (from notes)
docs.text:
• S01: Tsarina is apparently considering drafting people into army. War is coming. With whom?
  • S05: Situated between Grima and Tsarina palace.
  • S06: Audience with Tsarina. Dorphin is Duke of the Southeast. Clearly misleading Tsarina.
  • S07: Korb is the Tsarina's master armourer making my new bulette chain shirt, additional AC. A day to make. Free!
  • S07: We use our frog poison to try to make an impression. We pretend the Tsarina is about to get frog poisoned and save her last second. We think it worked.
  • S07: Convinced Tsarina we were friendly but forced to leave castle anyway, Karl's doing.
  • S18: Scry on Tsarina. She doesn't seem to be doing much in her palace.
  • S23: We journey southward towards Tsarina, and go to see Beurre the witch again. We tell her what we've learnt. She agrees to teleport us back to the Starter Tower above Grima. As soon as we arrive there with her, we are accosted by Karl and his cronies!
  • S24: Tsarina's house are Taiians, worshippers. Her family have been Rulers of Ym for 600 uncontested years.
  • S29: Back to Grima with plans to go kill the dragon (from the quest) then see the Tsarina.
  • S32: Grants her blessing for Bogsū to take holy orders, sends envoys to confirm Sverelt's ruin, and admits she has received Bogsū's letters but not read them.
  • S34: Grants the party her sigil to treat with the giants, then chokes on a red feather and is shaken by the idea that powers can affect reality.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Alayahâ€”spelled variously as Alaya, Eliah, Alaiya, or whatever Common decides todayâ€”is a druid who treats mysteries like ecosystems: map the predators, nurture the prey, and introduce a controlled burn where appropriate. They are patient with forests, impatient with tyrants, and uncommonly persuasive with eldritch entities that live behind suspicious windows. Ask five officials to write their name and youâ€™ll get six answers; Alayah seems content to let reality argue with itself while they get on with the work.

docs.title: Background & Drive
docs.text: Some druids wander until the world feels familiar. Alayah wandered until it felt strange enough. They collect patterns the way others collect trinkets: tides in politics, migration of rumours, and the way grief flows downhill through a city. The natural world taught them responsibility without supervision; the unnatural world taught them the joy of saying â€œnoâ€ to people who expected â€œyes.â€ Their compass swings toward places where power has pooled too longâ€”rotting wells that need opening, greedy roots that need pruning. Not crusading, exactly. Gardening, but for societiesâ€”and with more bears.

docs.title: Track Record (So Far)
docs.text: They spoke with sahuagin like neighbours over a fence and helped turn a krakenâ€‘egg â€œpearlâ€ crisis into an understanding rather than a shipwreck. They traced poison through a town to the ledger behind it, helping to unwind a green dragonâ€™s slowâ€‘motion asset strip. They haggled with a witch whose house has a sliding door and a sliding morality, and somehow left with future favours and intact eyebrows. At the palace, Alayah peered through a certain window into a pocketâ€‘reality, met Adonis Blue, and convinced him to paint a glamour over himself and stay quiet while the party moved the board. When the rest of us needed eyes in unseen rooms, Alayah scried, listened, and returned with maps the brave could actually use.

docs.title: Signature Abilities
docs.text:
• Wild Shape, Wise Shape: They turn into the right creature at the right timeâ€”scout, shield, or stampedeâ€”never spectacle for spectacleâ€™s sake.
  • Control & Care: Entangles fights until they behave; heals people until they remember theyâ€™re brave. Also excellent at reminding allies to drink water.
  • Investigative Rituals: If thereâ€™s a thread, Alayah will find it, label it, and hand it to you with tea. If there isnâ€™t, theyâ€™ll spin one from spider silk and stubbornness.
  • Diplomacy with Teeth: A calm voice that implies the forest is listeningâ€”and the forest is on their side.

docs.title: Quirks & Relationships
docs.text: They keep notes pressed with leaves, study city maps like river deltas, and consider hospitality a defensive spell. They trust Bogsūâ€™s centre of gravity, treat Bryceâ€™s charm like a renewable resource, and cheer Kaiâ€™s fireworks even when the drapes are flammable. Witches respect them. Bureaucrats worry about them. Both are correct. Also: if you spell their name â€œElia,â€ they will answer; if you spell it â€œAlaiah,â€ they will also answer; if you insist thereâ€™s a single correct spelling, they will smile the way trees smile at axes that forget to check their edge.

docs.title: Where They Shine
docs.text: When the obvious road has a toll you shouldnâ€™t pay, Alayah finds the path that touches moss and avoids traps. They are the partyâ€™s conscience and its caution, yesâ€”but also its audacity: the person who will knock on the universe and ask it to keep the noise down while we work. Offer them an impossible problem and they will compost it into three manageable ones, then invite you to plant something better in the space left over. The name on the wanted poster may vary; the results are remarkably consistent.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Bogsū started life as the partyâ€™s resident slab of resolveâ€”an honest fighter with a glaive, a prayer he hadnâ€™t quite learned yet, and a talent for getting in the way of danger so his friends didnâ€™t have to. Somewhere between bulette-chopping, windmill-de-slime-ing, and a few too many near misses, Taiia noticed. Now heâ€™s both hammer and hymn: a battlemaster who picked up a holy calling without putting the glaive down.

docs.title: Background & Drive
docs.text: Raised on simple promisesâ€”stand your post, mean what you say, pay what you oweâ€”Bogsū has always worn loyalty like armour. Heâ€™s the one who squares up to the awkward conversation, hands the trophy back to its rightful owner, and then quietly pays for the repairs. The Tsarinaâ€™s blessing to take holy orders didnâ€™t change him; it clarified him. Bogsū isnâ€™t angling for sainthood. Heâ€™s angling to make sure his people see the sunrise.

docs.title: Track Record (So Far)
docs.text: He has negotiated with sahuagin without losing fingers, helped unpoison a town that a fiscally responsible dragon was slowly asset-stripping, handed in monster parts without demanding a parade, and stood tall while court politics tried to make everyone shorter. When Alayah talked a reality-tuning founder into keeping a low profile, Bogsū made sure there was a profile to hide behind. At mass he knelt, listened, andâ€”miracle of miraclesâ€”rose a cleric, the warmth of Taiia settling into the cracks left by battle.

docs.title: Signature Abilities
docs.text:
• Battlemasterâ€™s Toolkit: Feints, ripostes, and the kind of controlled aggression that turns a crowded melee into a tidy queue.
  • New Light: Fresh clerical magicâ€”mending flesh, warding friends, and brandishing radiant answers to very rude questions.
  • Anchor Instincts: If the rest of the party is a kite in a storm, Bogsū is the stone that kindly refuses to fly.
  • Decent Humaning: People tell him the truth because he keeps giving it back to them, unvarnished and usable.

docs.title: Quirks & Relationships
docs.text: He side-eyes Michu on principle, respects Karl more than he admits, and collects improbable friends the way smithies collect burn marks. The Nameless Inn keeps his favourite chair ready; Korb keeps his measurements on file; Leon keeps a friendly eye on the glaiveâ€™s edge. Bogsū does not boast. He does, however, allow facts to be observed aloud in his vicinity.

docs.title: Where He Shines
docs.text: Put him between civilians and a problem and youâ€™ll see why gods court mortals. Bogsū shines when a path must be held, a promise must be kept, or a creature needs to realiseâ€”politely but firmlyâ€”that it has come to the wrong neighbourhood. Faith didnâ€™t tame him; it taught his strength a direction. Now the party has something rare: a wall that can sing.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Bryce Larkin is the swashbuckler who read the fine print and decided to write some new clauses. He wears trouble like a well-cut coat and has never met a window he didnâ€™t consider a door. If the party needs a smile at court, a blade in a stairwell, or a getaway planned three scenes in advance, Bryce is already thereâ€”probably with a quip, definitely with a plan.

docs.title: Background & Drive
docs.text: Born into the kind of family where â€œjewellerâ€ means half guild, half spy-ring, Bryce grew up learning the value of a flawless cutâ€”be it gem, reputation, or timing. Family obligations tug like tide: the search for answers around his father Linâ€™s captivity, the complicated debts that come with guild politics, and the quiet promise he keeps to those who didnâ€™t make it out. Under the panache is a stubborn line: no one gets to tell the story of his people without him in the room.

docs.title: Track Record (So Far)
docs.text: Bryce danced through sahuagin negotiations without getting damp, helped unpick the puzzle of a town sold out from under itself, and smiled pleasantly while very dangerous nobles measured his neck for metaphorical collars. He followed a trail of whispers from Vilaine toward Reichshaven, threaded through Yeeâ€™s keys and the Carniumâ€™s silences, and found momentum instead of despair. When the party steals a book, Bryce makes sure the consequences are what they planned to steal all along.

docs.title: Signature Abilities
docs.text:
• Blade & Footwork: Rapier precision, alleyway athleticism, and a nose for the one square no one is watching.
  • Social Acrobatics: Reads rooms like ledgers. Can launder a rumour in three questions or fewer.
  • Exit Strategy: If it has hinges, stairs, or gravity, Bryce will convert it into a strategy. Occasionally a hobby.
  • Shiny Things Sense: Not greedâ€”curation. The world puts secrets in boxes; Bryce insists on better boxes.

docs.title: Quirks & Relationships
docs.text: He is allergic to sloppy lies and enthusiastic incompetence. He keeps a professional fondness for thieves who have standards, a wary courtesy for monarchs who remember names, and a growing gratitude for a dragon bartender who doesnâ€™t ask questions he wouldnâ€™t answer himself. He teases Bogsū like a brother, trusts Alayahâ€™s instincts like a compass, and delights in Kaiâ€™s fireworks even when they go off during the sermon.

docs.title: Where He Shines
docs.text: Drop Bryce into moving politics and he swims. Give him a narrow ledge, and he dances. Heâ€™s the partyâ€™s vector: the line from â€œwe heard a rumourâ€ to â€œwe own the room,â€ all without losing the hat. The mission might be messy, but the ending? Bryce edits it until it sings.

docs.title: Overview
docs.text: Kai is a tiefling sorcerer with wild magic like weather: mostly sunny, occasionally dramatic, and once in a while the kind of lightning strike that gives a city a new statue. They do not so much solve problems as rearrange them until a solution falls out. The results are frequently beautiful and always memorable.

docs.title: Background & Drive
docs.text: Raised on a diet of sideways looks and sideways plans, Kai learned early that doing things the normal way meant doing them someone elseâ€™s way. Magic offered an alternativeâ€”one that was loud, colourful, and unreasonably effective. Theyâ€™re not chasing power so much as freedom: room to be themselves, and to make sure other people get the same chance no matter what their horns, histories, or hometowns look like.

docs.title: Track Record (So Far)
docs.text: Kai has assisted in diplomacy by being interesting enough that everyone briefly forgets war; survived negotiations with sahuagin by turning the vibe from â€œsharksâ€ to â€œsparklesâ€; and nearly behaved for the entire duration of a formal mass (a miracle, by all accounts). Theyâ€™ve helped stall a dragonâ€™s slow-burn crime, misdirected assassins with charm and smoke, and carried messages the universe didnâ€™t want deliveredâ€”then delivered them anyway.

docs.title: Signature Abilities
docs.text:
• Wild Magic Surges: Chaos with training wheels. Kai rides the wave and somehow lands on the right square.
  • Arcane Versatility: Illusion here, force there, a dash of teleport when the door is doing its best impression of a wall.
  • Performance Under Fire: If attention is a battlefield, Kai is artilleryâ€”dazzle, distract, and then the quiet knife arrives.
  • Empathic Edge: Finds the person in the monster and the joke in the tragedy, and uses both responsiblyâ€¦ish.

docs.title: Quirks & Relationships
docs.text: They fidget through sermons, collect compliments the way others collect coins, and name their tricks like pets. Kai adores Bryceâ€™s panache, borrows Alayahâ€™s patience (with interest), and treats Bogsū like a mobile safe zone. They flirt with danger and sometimes with bartenders. Results vary; stories donâ€™t.

docs.title: Where They Shine
docs.text: Give Kai a stalemate and theyâ€™ll manufacture a new set of rules. Need to exit a room that insists on being a room? Kai will convince it to be a hallway. They are the partyâ€™s spark plug and its pressure valve, and when the day needs colour, they bring a whole paintbox.

  • Buy a cart to head back south.
  • Solve a puzzle for the fae, get everlasting cigarette.
  • We journey by boat from Grima towards St Malia. Wrecked, washed up on an island. Pirates! We fuck them up.
  • Cut both main pirate's legs off so he talks. He sends us into village.
  • Habis is the town on a island. Tenris is the island leader baddie who we need to track down.

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
• Into the pub - the White Sheet
  • Tenris is the island leader. Mage. But apparently not on the island?
  • Storm coming from lighthouse. We believe there is a Dragon on the roof.
  • Elia climbed up as a spider, got spotted by dragon. Rest of us fight our way up but Elia nearly killed.
  • Kill the dragon down in the city hall. Grab his hoard, revive Elia.
  • Our ship is being repaired overnight

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
• Big fight with gnolls by the lake. We won.
  • Skinned the aurochs, take the steaks for the bounty.
  • Back to town to rest. Decide to investigate Isthmia stabbing.
  • Elia becomes a bloodhound.
  • Mrs Haingrove is a local witch. We found her cottage. She apparently bathes in blood to stay young. Wants the dagger Isthmia told us about.
  • Need to find Isthmium and bring him so witch can locate the sister.

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
• We learn that Lin Larkin, Bryce's dad (in-game), is the head of the jeweller's guild.
  • Larkin has been trading with Mrs Haingrove for blood and magic.
  • I donated a pint of blood to the witch.
  • Bryce gave a drop to help locate Lin.
  • Visit the Glub Glub Sahuagin home. Beautiful. Uncle Serpae remembers me and we chat.
  • Bogsu has learnt one word of their language: Pearl
  • Glubs have a huge orange pearl they are trying to sell.
  • Went to a trendy bar. Nearly drugged! Some kind of scam.
  • Bogsu confesses that IRL he works for a game company that owns the 'consumer' version of Faeblheim, which isn't lethal. He is a mole in this original version of the game to learn why people want to play it (the consumer verison is far less popular).
  • Qwertyuiop and Asdfghjkl - Bogsu's IRL bosses! - appear and tell us the world of Faeblheim is collapsing. Diamonds, resources, PCs all declining.
  • Need to investigate.
  • Demonic war that happened 3,000 years ago - in-game lore, never actually happened - is restarting somehow
  • We chase off the owner of the bar who tried to rob us, and make the barmaid the new manager.

docs.text:
• We argue about what this means. Decide to sail back to Grima, deliver pearl message.
  • Overhear on the boat - players talking about a Hermes key tournament.
  • The Labyrinth of Nightmare has a key in the middle of it.
  • Pierot is the french kobold on the boat. Player. Says he's level 12. Tells us where the labyrinth is, near the giant mountain.
  • We part ways with them.
  • Arrive at the jewellers guild. Say we're here about the sun pearl and have a message to deliver.

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
• Something awful has clearly happened at the Guild. We find a portal upstairs.
  • Try to go through a portal to Vilaine
  • Two cloaked assassins come straight through the portal and attack us.
  • Just about survived. Through to Vilaine.
  • Meet a huge group of players in the pub. We convince them of our cause, and to march on Karl.
  • A funky matrix wizard appears in the night and kills our brand new army of players! He seems to be able to manipulate reality in the game.
  • The group of players were all turned into gnolls, who chase us to the docks.

docs.text:
• Escape by boat. Headed to Hainic.
  • Awkward Reginald is the new captain of this boat. We try to enlist him.
  • Land on a geothermal island!
  • Weirdly, unseasonably warm
  • Learnt about Carnium Consortium. Local bandit mob. Trying to move into Haenic.
  • Mayor Clarice sends us off to the bandit camp.
  • Head out into forest. Very cold.

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
• We kill the bandits' dogs, but their snipers get away.
  • Spend the night in the forest eating horse.
  • Talk to a robin who turns out to be stupid and useless.
  • Continue to the temple. Built into the side of a mountain.
  • Slightly broken down palce. Can't see any sentries.
  • We wanted Elia to soul bond with his dog so he could relay intel to us from inside. But dog Int was only 3. So we put the headband of intellect on the dog. Suddenly a genius.
  • Agree on use of Schlortz code to communicate.
  • Elia becomes a spider and heads into the temple.
  • Relaying intel back to us but dog is just spouting philosophy.
  • We storm the temple. Bogsu am hasted.
  • Cargus Bloodbraided is their leader. We subdue him. I chop his hands off.
  • We demand that he show us downstairs.
  • Basement is barricaded shut and full of zombies.

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
• We stick Cargus' axes into his forearms so he can defend himself.
  • Down the stairs we go. Open the doors, kill some zombies.
  • Cargus tries to escape out another exit. Bryce chases him down just about leaving us downstairs. Bogsu and Bryce argue, Bogsu punches Bryce's lights out.
  • We have a rest in the dungeon dorm.
  • Bryce dreams about his wife. Her hands become shackles around his wrists.
  • He wakes up and immediately gets really drunk.
  • Sees his headband on the dog, chases it down, rips it off.
  • Grumpy Bryce. Bogsu and Elia head off for a brief dungeon crawl. Attacked by shadows in the basement kitchen and only just survive.
  • Bogsu and Bryce reconcile.
  • We return to Hainic. Meet mayor Clarice and give her Cargus' head.

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
• Reichhaven is our next target, back on the mainland.
  • Travel back past temple.
  • Ridiculously huge eagle soars overhead, doesn't come towards us.
  • Find an old witch Bheurre inside a cave with a sliding entrance. Seems friendly?
  • Been in game for a really long time.
  • We learn from her about the five players who started the game with founders Hiki and Yukimori.
  • She says she will give us five scryings if we agree to try and steal two roc eggs for her.
  • Shows us Vilaine first. Place seems damaged but mostly ok. Inn we were in is in ruins. Lots of flowers laid out but not as many as there were dead players.
  • We scry on Lin, Father of Bryce. We see jailer Yee interrogate him.
  • We also scry on Carl. In the mayoral residence. He is sitting and writing. We manage to see his journal!
  • Head out to find the roc. Attacked by kobolds on the way, make short work of them.
  • We strap a bottle of the frog whisky to one and send him in. Roc eats him, turns into frog, which we catch in a jar. Grab the eggs and two feathers.

• Tracking down the Consortium. We enter a vamp hole. Go down tunnel.

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
• Bogsu and friendly Goliath Czarel team up to survive. Attempt to save Czarel's friends and family, kidnapped by Gnoll war party.
  • Both get captured, but rescued by rest of party!
  • We need to kill Ashmogh. He is the leader of the gnolls and is doing a ritual to summon demon god Yeenoghu. This is the demon war happening again.
  • Ashmogh is in Me-Na. Czarel tells us Ashmogh will fight fair if we challenge him one on one.
  • Killing Ashmogh would end the ritual to summon Yeenoghu
  • We send a message to Leon the metallic dragon. He will come meet us in Me-Na.
  • [Cut scene: No he won't! Murdered from afar by digital magic once again! We don't see this, DM narrates]

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
• Shake Czarel's hand and send him off. Good luck bud, I hope we meet again.
  • Ashmogh had a Hermes Key on him.
  • We use it to send Lin Larkin/Bryce's wife out of the game. Bryce has a book with him that lets him communicate with the real world. He asks it if his wife has reappeared. Seems like a yes.
  • A weird guy turns up while we rest. Possibly game founder or developer? Adonis Blue, "Dagger".
  • He has mad powers. Feeds us knockout booze.
  • Can manipulate reality. Has a crazy magic staff. Threatens us gently and disappears.
  • We journey southward towards Tsarina, and go to see Beurre the witch again. We tell her what we've learnt. She agrees to teleport us back to the Starter Tower above Grima. As soon as we arrive there with her, we are accosted by Karl and his cronies!

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
• Parley with the Player Killers.
  • "We have a middle ground person." "You have...a little brown person???"
  • Suggest we all drink truth Water so we can all be frank bu Karl isn't keen.
  • Karl: early versions of game were an alpha. Demon war was part of the history of the game.
  • When v1.5 was released the game became easier, softer.
  • Agree to a truce. They will fight with us, they will provide a sending stone.
  • Boss served Bogsu a really rubbish banana daiquiri.
  • Bogse eyeballs Michu throughout. Going to kill him one day.
  • They bamf out of existence when we're done talking. We go up to the library.
  • Looking for books on bananas. Find Baphomet.
  • Yeenoghu v Baphomet was the basis of the war. Baphomet likely the instigator. Loves putting terror into people.
  • Great Goddess who stepped in to end the war: Taiia.
  • Taiia is the Goddess who made the world. Periods where she's awake and asleep at the same time? When she sleeps the demons fought. Asleep again now?
  • Shrines to Taiia - could we visit one?
  • The other three players watch the game's explainer VR video about the first demon war in sort-of-vr. Bogsū stays out reading in the library.
  • In the video, they are in a labyrinth while Baphomet's Beasts attack. Elia becomes a giraffe to see over the wall.
  • The Worldspire is the mountain in the centre of the continent. Much smaller in the ancient intro video!
  • Find a book saying Hermes keys can be made from a combo of tiefling horn, elf heart, and human brain... This seems likely to be a veiled contract on our heads! Who knows if it's even true.
  • Tsarina's house are Taiians, worshippers. Her family have been Rulers of Ym for 600 uncontested years.
  • We jump out of the Starter Tower. Something is wrong though. We can see Grima but also a hell dimension racing up towards us. We try to aim towards Grima.
  • Only the dog fails the save and starts falling into hell. Bogsu saves it with his glaive of returning. The dog loses a leg. The leg falls into hell.

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
• We leave Boss' forge. He is working on my glaive and a robot leg for the dog.
  • Busy Horse Exchange. Lots of adventurers looking for jobs, vibe of something happening, nerves.
  • Quests available: Squids In, Claw and Hoarder, Horn This Way, No Room To Swing A Catoblepas, Slimes They Are A Changing, Field of Study. A Pit To Hiss In.
  • Now you can make a Hermes key out of tiefling horns and lungs and heart.
  • In the place, a bunch of adventurers want to hunt tieflings. Kai makes herself known. They get shirty.
  • Kai and Aliya leave towards the docks. Bogsū and Bryce follow.
  • Glub Glub mission accidentally starts. Huge squid quest. Kraken appears.
  • Glub glubs have taken the sun pearl. In danger of fight v players and kraken at the same time.
  • Other players run away. Kraken is L23. Sun pearl isn't sun pearl, it's a kraken egg. Just about survived. But hey we got the Squids In reward.
  • Up to a windmill for the slimes quest. It's a mill where they feed them rubbish and they produce power.
  • At the mill. Tilla Munchen opens the door.
  • Loads of slimes inside. We start blasting.

  • Quests available: Squids In, Claw and Hoarder, Horn This Way, No Room To Swing A Catoblepas, Slimes They Are A Changing, Field of Study. A Pit To Hiss In.
  • Now you can make a Hermes key out of tiefling horns and lungs and heart.
  • In the place, a bunch of adventurers want to hunt tieflings. Kai makes herself known. They get shirty.
  • Kai and Aliya leave towards the docks. Bogsū and Bryce follow.
  • Glub Glub mission accidentally starts. Huge squid quest. Kraken appears.
  • Glub glubs have taken the sun pearl. In danger of fight v players and kraken at the same time.
  • Other players run away. Kraken is L23. Sun pearl isn't sun pearl, it's a kraken egg. Just about survived. But hey we got the Squids In reward.
  • Up to a windmill for the slimes quest. It's a mill where they feed them rubbish and they produce power.
  • At the mill. Tilla Munchen opens the door.
  • Loads of slimes inside. We start blasting.

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
• Big fight against the slimes. One huge one. We win fairly easily. My courtesy Halberd from Boss gets melted,
  • Get paid 3000 gold.
  • Back into the centre of town. To the Dawn Chapel.
  • Two Taian clerics giving a dead tiefling last rites. Its horns have been chopped off....
  • They give me literature, a blessing, and a symbol of Taia on a wooden plaque. We thank them and leave. Kai does a lot of sniggering.
  • Alayah listens in to some chat at a bar, disguised as a spider. Three adventurers in a group talking about IRL stuff, another group of assassins talking about in-world work.
  • We return to Bryce's Thieves Guild apartment to sleep. Bogsu stays up to darn the symbol onto his demon armour.
  • Bogsu is out early to go to the service at the Dawn Chapel. Father Obuasi Acca does the service. Nice guy. Welcomes me. Tells me he prayed for me last night - goddess Taia appeared and spoke to him about me. The most clearly he has ever seen her.
  • Taia told him of three sites - shrines. One in the centre of the maze. The spine of the world. And another mysterious one.
  • Back to Boss. Hard at work on weapon and dog leg. I pay for the broken halberd, and buy another outright. Back to collect things tomorrow.

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
• New +3 Glaive picked up. It is sweet.
  • We are making plans to head into the Darmstadt forest to look for the maze mission.
  • Ask the Zoological Society for advice on where a Howler was found.
  • Giant Badger Elia digs us a hole, we make a shelter, it is rainy. Miserable night on the edge of the forest near the sea cliff.
  • Elia talks to plants. They want us to enter the maze and die. Clearly Baphomet is now in charge rather than the fey.
  • Leshen comes at us in the morning. In our camp. We trick it into falling in our pit.
  • We kill it fairly easily and short rest. Clearly the forest wants to kill casters first.
  • We journey into the maze.
  • DM intervention on eating wild shaped druid: "I'm laying down the law. If someone eats an Aurochs rump steak while you're wild shaped, your character is missing an ass cheek."
  • Find a ruined camp. Contains some stuff. Nothing too cool.
  • We press on. Maze is clearly fucking with us.
  • Then suddenly we're in a clearing at the centre. It is the Green Thingy, the pub we came to first time in the forest.
  • Place is deserted but for four low level PCs. They are terrified. No sign of Leon...
  • We long rest upstairs. In the morning, growling from downstairs. We descend to find a panther wolf thing.... End of ep.

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
• We beat the disco moose/hunter thing. It was hard as nails.
  • It leaves behind half the face-mask of Baphomets general, known as Anghraboddh
  • We had a long chat with L2 player character Aeschyl, who was in the tavern. Good guy. Convince him to head to Grima to try and achieve something useful.
  • Find a golden dragon egg in the hearth in the Green Thingy! Cool to the touch. We take it with us.
  • Back to Grima with plans to go kill the dragon (from the quest) then see the Tsarina.
  • Stash the egg in Bryce's place and leave.
  • Take the boat to where the green dragon is for the next quest - Harz
  • Pub in town. Play cards with a fisherman. Barmaid tells us the green dragon is basically an accountant. Looks after gold but doesn't give it back?
  • Lee-Kin Knightly is the quest giver.

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
• Harz tavern is called Cinnamon Sticks. In we go.
  • A big clumpy noise from door across the hall. Intriguing.
  • Barmaid tells us nothing about the resident. Clearly hiding something. Name is Annie. Another town, Teremin, nearby, has more trouble with the dragon.
  • We buy grog and drink.
  • Elia sneaks into the clumpy room as a spider to investigate. Bryce follows all sneaky. Coffin instead of a bed? That was the clump noise.
  • Along the eastern Ym coast we travel. Chilly. Towards Teremin for intel on dragon.
  • Near the dragons lair, we pull out the magic boat and drift downriver towards Teremin.
  • Town has a plume of black smoke rising. A pyre.
  • Meet an old man. Johannes Skeinseth. Whole village decimated by poisoned water flowing from the dragon cave. Big burning pits.
  • We ask him and his horse to take us up river.
  • At the dragon cave are two hill giants. We sneak up and fall upon them. One goes down quick, the other flees.
  • A Sahuagin maiden is sent out to talk to us. She's been ensorcelled by the dragon and isn't much use. We send her and it sounds like she gets eaten.
  • We tempt the dragon to the mouth of its cave. It blasts us with poison. We chase it in.
  • Do a lot of damage but it's nails. Knocks down Bogsū and Bryce, then flees into a pool that leads back into its lair. We short rest and prep to follow it in.

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
• We decide to go into the cave after the dragon.
  • Elia becomes a plesiosaur. "You can both ride me - I'm large."
  • Bryce and Bogsū get ready to ride the druid plesiosaur into the grotty lake.
  • Kai sneaks in through a tight tunnel to find the large internal cave. Drops a glowing mushroom into the water. We see it from outside, presumably so does the dragon. Dragon emerges and breath-attacks Kai who goes down fast.
  • Plesiosaur plus fighters burst out of water just as the dragon goes to eat Kai.
  • Bogsū pops a flying potion. We hit the dragon hard, trip attack, she falls into the pool.
  • Wel follow her into the next chamber. Bryce nearly dies.
  • We kill her, get loads of treasure, including two 'glitched' items - more evidence of the game breaking down. Elia tries to attune to one of them just to see...
  • Adonis Blue/Dagger appears in response. Hates anything inauthentic in the game. 'Deletes' the two glitched items and gets angry when we mention anything about the outside world. He just wants to reset the game, free of players - but suggests he might be convinced if we say we are on the side of authenticity/honesty in-game. Storms off and disappear.
  • We have a long chat. Bryce wants to go home to his wife but vows to help the rest of the party first. Bogsu is upset by this, says Bryce should just exit as soon as we find a Hermes key.
  • In Bryce's diary, a message in response to a question he had written ('Where can I find what I seek?'. Three locations - Maze of Nightmares, Stone Giant Trial, Ceran Palace....
  • We had resolved to go to the Palace next, where Bogsu may learn how the Tsarina has been receiving his regular letters of love. Also time to level up.

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
• After a rest we head back towards Teremin
  • Mayor thanks us, place looks better already.
  • Onward to Harz and to the Cinnamon Sticks.
  • White skinned elf receives the briefcase. Lee-Kin Knightly. Pays us. Mysterious ice queen - surely part of the carnium.
  • I pay Cronin, ex military orc. Pay him a bit to tail her, just as she walks out. Good luck orcy guy.
  • Annie asks us to leave, we refuse as we've paid for our night. Tense night in our room, very quiet, but nothing happens and we leave.
  • We see Cronin. He tells us he saw someone fat sneaking out out towards Teremin...
  • Alayah scries on the gravedigger Johannes. Sat in a room with the mayor, and Lee-Kin...
  • Briefcase is on the table. Contains deeds to every building in Teremin.
  • Travel to Floros. Karl is here! We have some good chat. He is considering banning the killing of tieflings.
  • Karl goes in to talk to the Tsarina. We go to some rooms to freshen up.
  • Karl knocks in Bogsus door. Tsarina wants to see Alayah about the window quest.
  • We have an audience with her. Goes ok. We convince her we're not baddies and a war is happening. Tell her about the prophecy. She gives her blessing to Bog taking holy orders.
  • Has received his letters...but not read them yet!
  • She sends envoys to Sveralt to confirm it's ruined.
  • We chat with Korb and Boss. I give back the Buleit carapace armour.
  • Alayah investigates the window. Adonis Blue is out there in his pocket universe. Alayah convinces him to hide himself with a glamour and the game kind of blurs out the quest for Alayah?
  • Time for mass. We all attend. Kai just about manages to sit quietly.
  • Bogsu says a silent prayer and feels the spirit of Taiia enter him. He is a cleric!

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
• Bogsū is sad that the Tsarina has not been reading his letters.
  • The party visits the Cathedral of Taiia and speaks with Deacon Fned about faith and the mask of Anghraboddh.
  • Fned says the mask is immensely powerful and can be destroyed by a Wish spell or by casting it into the Great Forge in the Infinite Maze of Baphomet.
  • In the palace maze, the party is drawn into a hellish maze filled with fog, lightning, sulphur and demon dogs.
  • A terrified royal soldier, Ahmed, joins the party but is later crushed during the chaos.
  • The party meets a strange dark-skinned original player/shopkeeper who wants magic items in exchange for sending them home.
  • Alayah's magic beans become ten magical toads, which then become a genie and a string of dangerous creatures, including a balor, a silver dragon, xorn and a cloud giant.
  • The shopkeeper uses the genie to remove the monsters and send the party home.
  • Back in the centre of the palace maze, the party uncovers an altar and writings about the Second Cataclysm, the God-Felling Star and Taiia's six champions or saints.
  • Inside the cracked altar, Eliyah finds another altar with more figures. Blue human hands grab her antennae and heal her, while voices ask for help.
  • Deacon Fned identifies the outer figure as Saint Carafect and the trapped inner figures as Saint Corellon and Saint Rillifane.
  • The party returns the next day with Fned, Army Commander Ject and soldiers, but learns little more.

docs.text: • Destroy_the_Mask_of_Anghraboddh

docs.title: Source Notes
docs.text:
• Start in our rooms. Bogsū sad the Tsarina isn't reading his letters.
  • Heading out to the maze again.
  • Barracks, training ground, petting zoo, amphitheatre, library.
  • Off to the Cathedral of Taiia. Two orderlies tidying the place up after morning service.
  • Deacon Fned is in charge. Chat to him about faith.
  • Ask him about the mask of Anghraboddh.
  • It's very powerful. We should destroy it? Has to go in the fires of hell?
  • The Mask of Anghraboddh is our sudden new mission for the future. It can be destroyed with a Wish spell, or in the Infinite Maze of Baphomet by casting it into the Great Forge.
  • We head to the maze in the gardens.
  • Suddenly the maze gets weird. Fog. Lightning. Sulphur. Suddenly in hell?
  • Demon dog attacks, we kill it fast.
  • Royal soldier, terrified, appears. Ahmed. Scared and lost. Joins us. I take his spear.
  • Kill three more dogs eating the corpse of another soldier.
  • Follow blood trail. Meet a strange black-skinned guy in a makeshift tent. He is clearly some sort of original player. He asks for magic items to get us home.
  • Alayah busts out the magic beans. They become ten magic toads.
  • The toads are magic. We touch one. It becomes a genie. Bogsū accidentally immediately gives it to the strange guy.
  • Alayah decides to touch all the toads at once. One becomes a balor. The next becomes a sahuagin. The next becomes a grimlock. Next an adult silver dragon. Then a merfolk. Then a pegasus. A xorn. A giant rat. Another xorn. A cloud giant.
  • We beg the shopkeeper to help us. He uses the genie to get rid of all the monsters. He sends us home. Ahmed not with us, he was crushed.
  • We are suddenly back out in the centre of the maze. Back in the prime.
  • There is magic below us.
  • There is text when we dig. We read about the Second Cataclysm and the God-Felling Star. A history of when Taiia split herself into six champions to fight the GFS, or six mortals came together to fight. Less well documented than the Demon War. Also caused St Malia to split from the mainland.
  • We gather up the paper and the marble stone with the story written on it. Turns out to be an altar. Has a figure wearing a cloak of feathers.
  • Faezel is the GFS and the author?
  • The marble altar is full of cracks. We drop in a pebble with Light cast on it. Eliyah becomes an ant and crawls in. Sees another altar inside, with more figures inside.
  • Suddenly something grabs Eliyah's ant antennae. Blue human hands! Eliyah healed to full health somehow.
  • Voice says help.
  • Guy with hand outstretched was on the internal altar.
  • Bogsū tries to get a vibe from the altar. Hears voices saying "Help us" and another responding "No."
  • We decide to return to the church and the Deacon.
  • Outer: Saint Carafect.
  • Saint Corellon and Saint Rillifane are the two trapped on the inside altar.
  • There were six saints. There were six monarchs.
  • We plan to return there tomorrow with the Deacon. We ask the army commander to put a few soldiers together to come with us.
  • Army Commander Ject is going with us with five soldiers.
  • The altar is still there. Very weird but we learn nothing. No clue but the Deacon is perturbed. We leave the maze again.

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
• The party is summoned to breakfast with the Tsarina and discusses strategy.
  • The palace will train peasants into soldiers.
  • The Tsarina grants the party her sigil as proof of her authority and sends them to treat with the giants.
  • In a private meeting with Bogsū, the Tsarina begins choking on a red feather and becomes frightened that powers can affect reality.
  • The party heads north towards the Spire and sends a bird messenger to Bryce's new guild, asking them to watch the door to his house.
  • On the way through high mountain country, storms gather around the plains.
  • In a town near the Spire, the party meets Friar Blidd, whose companions were murdered by goblins.
  • Blidd is somehow compromised. After he bites Kai, the party realises the problem may be lycanthropy.

docs.title: Source Notes
docs.text:
• We are summoned to breakfast with the Tsarina.
  • Lot of discussion about strategy. Palace will train peasants into soldiers.
  • We ask for her own sigil to have her authority. She grants it.
  • Sends us off to treat with the giants.
  • Bog summoned to meeting with Tsarina. She starts to choke on a red feather. Suddenly terrified that there are powers that can affect reality. Sends us on our way.
  • Head towards Spire. Decide to send a message to Bryce's new guild to watch the door to his house. Sent on a druid bird messenger.
  • Bogsū tries to tuck the red feather into her hair. She doesn't go for it.
  • Start making our way north towards the spire. High up, mountainous. Giant country.
  • Clouds and storm roll in as we head up the plains. Looks rough. A seasonal regular elemental, known to the area.
  • Meet Friar Blidd in town. All his mates murdered by goblins. Big storms all around.
  • Offer to help by killing goblins.
  • Friar Blidd was somehow compromised.
  • Kai is bitten by Blidd. Oh shit, werewolf.

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
• The party nears the last town before the plains and meets the Reinhardts, refugees from Sverelt led by Arigal.
  • The Spire's main city has a gambling tournament, but people who go there have not returned.
  • Bogsū wins money in an epic game of Liar's Dice.
  • The party shops with the brewer and buys another corrupted item.
  • Etzba, the town at the base of the Spire, is full of giants.
  • The party learns that Queen Murgata has been murdered and Regent Murgato is the new leader.
  • The party begins investigating the spate of murders.
  • Autopsies show huge slashes to the throat and signs that something burst from the chest or back.
  • A small giant child witnessed a murder.
  • At a crime scene, Eliyah learns from a lizard that a ghostly thing appeared, disappeared and reappeared.

docs.title: Source Notes
docs.text:
• We are making our way towards the Spire.
  • Nearing the last town before the plains.
  • See a circle of wagons: the Reinhardts, approach seems chill.
  • Arigal is their leader, we are welcomed.
  • Refugees from Sverelt.
  • Spire main city has a gambling tournament but people who have gone there have not returned.
  • We have an epic game of Liar's Dice. Bogsū wins money off everyone.
  • Bit of shopping from the brewer.
  • We buy another corrupted item.
  • Arigal wishes us well.
  • In the morning we make for the Spire. Huge, like the Shard but with a base two miles across. Sheer walls.
  • Etzba is the town at the base of the Spire. Giants milling about.
  • Chat with a giant as we arrive: spate of murders.
  • Regent Murgato is the new leader of the giants after Queen Murgata was murdered.
  • Quest: Mystery Giants Theatre.
  • Start to investigate. To the graveyard. Gravedigger answers questions.
  • Corpse shows huge slashes to throat. Also something has burst from the chest? We cut around.
  • Autopsy suggests neck slash and chest burst from behind.
  • Meanwhile, K and B go to meet small giant child murder witness. Evidence the same.
  • We go to the crime scene in an alley. E talks to a local lizard. Heard about the murder second hand. Sounds accurate. Ghostly thing that disappeared and reappeared.

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
• The party hears many giant stories and legends from their imp.
  • They travel to Cafregil to continue investigating murdered giants.
  • Emory, a city guide, helps them navigate the giant city.
  • The party visits a giant tavern, then the small-people bar called The Escape.
  • In The Escape, a dragonborn and a half-orc fight near a strange portal; the half-orc gets an arm stuck in the portal and it has to be cut off.
  • The party meets old friends from the boat, also present for the tournament.
  • Bogsū duels the half-orc and wins by a single hit point.
  • Bogsū prays at the temple, is dragged into darkness by a psychic killer and nearly dies.
  • The others find a kobold murdered in an alley and rush to rescue Bogsū at the temple.
  • Emory identifies the staring face in the party's night visions as the Megurash, evil spirits from long ago that now threaten the giants again.

docs.title: Source Notes
docs.text:
• Lots of giant stories and legends told by our imp.
  • Cafregil is the big giant city. We head there to do more autopsies on murdered giants.
  • Reach the city, speak to guards.
  • City is a sort of underground warren built above ground. Here as first line of defence for Spire.
  • Meet Emory, a city guide.
  • We head to the giant tavern first. We feel little.
  • Don't learn a lot. Head to small people part of town and into a bar: The Escape.
  • Up on the balcony a dragonborn and half-orc are fighting near a weird portal. Orc gets arm stuck in portal and has to have it chopped off.
  • Everyone excited for the tournament.
  • We meet our old friends from the boat. They are here for the tournament too.
  • Me and the half-orc agree to a quick duel to warm up. Bogsū wins by a single hit point. Lots of mutual respect.
  • Bogsū goes for a bit of a pray at the temple in town, still pretty knocked up. Kneels and prays. Opens eyes and all is dark. Psychic killer attacks him and down he goes.
  • The others find the kobold murdered in an alley, no sign of Bogsū. They race to the temple.
  • Rescue Bogsū from near death, gash down his back.
  • Turns out the kobold was long dead: five hours ago. Clearly was someone or something else.
  • At night we get visions of a staring face. Emory tells us it is the Megurash.
  • They are evil spirits from long ago. Threatening the giants again now.
  • We prepare to head up the Spire.

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
• After shopping, the party begins climbing the Spire.
  • Stone giant guards suggest that the Tsarina's seal should grant some recognition, but that Murgato will be less respected higher up the Spire.
  • The settlement inside the Spire is called Orimdal, a dwarven hollow of platforms, rope bridges and 120,000 steps.
  • The party observes the fighting tournament and learns at least one combatant was murdered before competing.
  • Karl and his crew are also at the tournament.
  • Tournament attendees begin dying from poisoned food.
  • After two hours of climbing, the party meets Bobo, the head monkey massager.
  • The party must choose whether to keep climbing or use the internal elevator.

docs.title: Source Notes
docs.text:
• After a spot of shopping, we head up the Spire.
  • Last chat with stone giant guards. Sounds like we should get some recognition with the Tsarina's seal.
  • Murgato likely to be less respected as we climb. Suggests we treat frost giants frostily, fire giants firely, etc.
  • Up through the Spire we climb. Clearly hollowed out by dwarves and still a lot running around. Settlement is called Orimdal.
  • Platforms, rope bridges etc. rising up.
  • Slow progress up the 120,000 steps. We take a break to observe the fighting tournament. At least one combatant had been murdered before competing.
  • Karl and co are here.
  • People at the tournament are dying from poisoned food.
  • After two hours climbing, met Bobo, head monkey massager.
  • Choice about whether to climb or use internal elevator.

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
• In flashback, the party speaks to Kamu and Wilde, who are taking Pierot's body away from the Spire but still plan to fight in the tournament.
  • Back in the present, the party travels by dwarf-driven wagon through a tiny tunnel to Orimdal.
  • Orimdal is a geothermal settlement of stone buildings cut into the walls, rope bridges, lava below and nethercite ore travelling upward.
  • Kompir, the Head Smith, explains that the dwarves make armour for local giants in return for housing.
  • The party proceeds towards the fire giant citadel of Levragel.
  • Outside Levragel, chained dead monsters come to life while fire giants battle other huge creatures.
  • The party joins the fight against an undead dragon and hydra.
  • Eliyah polymorphs the undead dragon into a snail before being petrified, and Kai saves stone elephant Eliyah from sliding into lava.

docs.title: Source Notes
docs.text:
• Flashback to earth giant city two episodes ago. Talking to Kamu and Wilde. They are about to take the body of Pierot away from the Spire but still planning to fight in the tournament.
  • Back to the present: in the Orimdal pub.
  • Turns out Orimdal is the town higher up the Spire. Nethercite ore from down here gets sent up there.
  • Up the wagon through a tiny tunnel for a day. Wagon driven by dwarf, pulled by a mule. Uneventful.
  • Arrived in Orimdal. Geothermal, stone buildings cut into the walls.
  • Rope bridges over the gap, lava below.
  • Kompir is the Head Smith.
  • Long chat. They make lots of armour for local giants in return for housing.
  • We decide to proceed via the fire giant citadel of Levragel. It will be hot.
  • The fire giants have huge magical creatures hanging from chains outside the citadel. Sacrifices or rituals.
  • Up to Levragel. It is fucking hot. Looking down is a sea of lava. Huge rocky plateau houses Levragel. Houses made of solidified molten rock, misshapen.
  • As we step out, the chained dead monsters have come to life. Various fire giants fighting big monsters.
  • We join the fight with an undead dragon. Elia polymorphs it into a snail, just before being petrified.
  • The dog drops the snail over the edge. It hits the lava, dies as a snail, turns around to fly back at us.
  • Kill the dragon and the hydra. Stone elephant Elia sliding towards edge! Kai dimension doors it to safety.

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
• The party helps clean up the undead creatures around Levragel.
  • A roc flies overhead; the party attacks it and the fire giants finish it off.
  • Chieftain Enora, leader of the fire giants, is aware of the Megurash but surprised by the dead creatures awakening.
  • Enora's cleric unpetrifies Eliyah, and the party is given a human-sized guest lodge.
  • A fire giant water-bearer melts Bogsū's gold piece in his hand and pours it out as a bracelet for him.
  • At Enora's banquet, the food withers on the plate and the party is attacked.
  • Enora runs down the corridor and disappears.
  • The party fights and kills a crawling undead creature that summons skeletons.
  • Bogsū tries to persuade Enora while on 4 hp; she tells him to climb the Spire, defeat the ancient evil, and catch her on the way down.

docs.title: Source Notes
docs.text:
• Clean up undead creatures.
  • Roc flies overhead, we attack it.
  • Fire giants finish it off. We go to meet their chieftain.
  • Chieftain Enora. Aware of Megurash. But surprised by dead creatures awakening.
  • Good chat. She wants to dine with us later. Her cleric unpetrifies Elia.
  • We're led to a lodge. Guest house for human-sized people.
  • Bogsū steps out. Asks for water. Gets brought. Offers gold piece as thanks. Water bringer giant laughs, takes gold, melts it in hand. Pours it out as bracelet for Bogsū. Banging.
  • Called to banquet. Wearing fancy clothes as we go.
  • Good talk. Explain the murders downstairs and the state of the nation.
  • Food served. Immediately withers on the plate. Attack!
  • Chieftain runs down the corridor, disappears.
  • Big undead crawling thing. Summons skeletons. We fight it. Kill it.
  • Back to lodgings to heal before moving on.
  • Bogsū goes to Chieftain's chambers, knocks. Says he has 4 hp and a potion of growth and one night in town. Roll for persuasion: 2. Roll again: 3.
  • Chieftain says, hand me the potion. Climb the Spire. Defeat this ancient evil. Catch me on the way down.

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
• The party leaves the fire giant town and emerges outside into cold.
  • They arrive at Gufragel, the frost giant town, and go to the Eynhall.
  • Jarl Stoor gives them a cold reception but enjoys the rivalry between the party and Karl's crew, who arrived by elevator.
  • From Gufragel, the party can see across Ymleif as far as Grima.
  • Jarl Stoor reports lost scouts around the mountain, possibly due to yeti.
  • Eliyah scries on the scouts using giant cloth and sees corpses, blood and footprints that do not leave the area.
  • Bogsū buys a cloth that gives +1 AC when he is above 75% health.
  • At the site of the dead scouts, the party experiences hallucinations and retreats to a stone shack.
  • The party fights a Megurash on the roof, with Bogsū badly hurt and then polymorphed into a giant ape.

docs.title: Source Notes
docs.text:
• We leave the fire giant town. Through the tunnels to an exit and we're suddenly outside and cold again.
  • Frost giant town. Built-up area ahead. Frost giants lumbering around.
  • Gufragel is the town. To the Eynhall.
  • The elders eye us up. Cold reception.
  • We stare out over the continent of Ymleif, can see all the way to Grima.
  • Frost giant king guy has lighter skin, tattoos, dreads. Jarl Stoor.
  • Fucking Karl and co are here! Took the elevator.
  • We argue with them about who gets to go to the top of the Spire.
  • Jarl reckons our rivalry has the makings of a classic tapestry saga.
  • Jarl tells us some scouts have been lost around the mountain. Possibly yeti.
  • Elia goes scrying with giant cloth. Frost giant scouts. On the mountain pass. Pools of blood. Footprints, not giant. Not leaving the area.
  • The cloth giant lady sells us some cloth. Bogsū buys one that gives +1 AC when above 75% health.
  • Find the corpses at the spot Elia scried. Suddenly shit gets weird. Bryce hallucinates something. We stand back to back. Make our way to a little shack.
  • Big awful fight with Megurash. We light up the shack but the fight happens on the stone roof. Bogsū hit hard. Elia polymorphs him into a giant ape.

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
• The party kills Midnakesh and its ghostly servants, though one spirit escapes.
  • Back in Gufragel, the party learns that Garsay is the storm giant queen and that they should show deference.
  • The party visits Karl's crew, plays dice and wins money from them.
  • Bogsū shows Boss the horn. Boss appraises it as pure magical fear.
  • The party grinds a chunk of the horn into vodka and drinks it, producing awful visions for nearly everyone.
  • The next morning, the party takes a wagon towards the storm giants, watching Karl's wagon ahead.
  • They arrive in Rosh, the storm giant citadel: floating plateaus, bridges, minarets and carved stone.
  • At the top of the Spire, they see a huge glyph of Taiia: the beginning of her spell, the end of the Demon War, waiting for activation.
  • Rosh is bent and broken, with storm giant bones everywhere and razor leaves growing from corpses.

docs.title: Source Notes
docs.text:
• Really hard fight but we kill Midnakesh and his ghostly goons. One of them floats off.
  • We go to sleep in the stone shack and heal up.
  • Wake up in the morning and look around.
  • We return to Gufragel.
  • Garsay is the storm giant queen. Advises us to kowtow.
  • Before bed we nip into Karl and co's room. Playing dice. We win a bunch of money from them.
  • Bogsū shows Boss the horn. He appraises it as pure magical fear. We break off a chunk, grind it up, mix in vodka and do a shot each.
  • All get awful trips. Their crew as bad as ours. Elia resists.
  • Night time.
  • In the morning we get on a wagon up to the storm giants: two days' journey. Keep an eye on the Karl wagon ahead of us.
  • Arrive in Rosh. Storm giant citadel. Carved stone, minarets, spires. Spread across floating plateaus connected by bridges.
  • At the very peak of the Spire, a huge 10 km glyph floating in the sky. Taiia! The beginning of her spell, the end of the Demon War, waiting for activation.
  • But the city is bent and broken. Bones of storm giant everywhere. Razor leaves growing out of corpses.

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
• The party enters the broken storm giant city and splits from Karl's crew.
  • They encounter multiple monsters while moving through Rosh.
  • Taiia's power is being sapped into an altar at the central palace, preventing Bogsū from properly reaching her.
  • The party meets Alvus, a near-dead cloud giant, and heals him enough for him to join them.
  • The party kills the resurrected Hunt Master and another giant riding a behir.
  • After resting, the party sees Anghraboddh, General of Baphomet, ahead: a huge deer-skull demon.
  • The party prepares to enter his maze.

docs.title: Source Notes
docs.text:
• Enter the giant city.
  • Split from Karl and co: they're not keen to get into trouble.
  • Bunch of encounters with various monsters based on a random BM table.
  • Taiia is being impeded at the central palace somehow. Her power being sapped into an altar.
  • When Bogsū tries to speak to her, gets interrupted somehow.
  • Meet Alvus, a nearly dead cloud giant. Tells us about the tragedies the city has suffered. Joins our party after we heal him a bit.
  • We kill the Hunt Master, who had been resurrected. We also kill a giant on a behir.
  • After a short rest we note that up ahead is Anghraboddh, General of Baphomet. Deer skull on a big demon guy. Huge.
  • We prep to enter his maze.

docs.title: Summary
docs.text:
• The party battles Anghraboddh through two stages: first with a huge bone scythe, then with horns that become daggers.
  • Anghraboddh dissolves when killed.
  • Ahead, an altar is sucking power from the top of the Spire.
  • Dagger is revealed as the mastermind behind the altar and the draining of Taiia's power.
  • The party tries to talk him out of ruining the world again, but he wants to be alone in a cleansed Faeblheim.
  • Dagger raises the Ceran Palace a hundred feet into the air on a stone platform, like another Worldspire.
  • Dagger is too powerful, but Karl's crew arrives and helps the party hold him off. Boss loses an arm to necrotic damage.
  • The party survives long enough to crack the altar.
  • A Saint spills out and says he is one of the Monarchs, and so is Dagger.
  • The party learns that they need to free the other Saints to stand any chance against Dagger.
  • One Saint is in Grima, one is elsewhere, and the last two are in the Ceran Palace itself.
  • The party levels up and each receives an item that lets them long rest inside two turns.

• We battle Anghraboddh. He is nails but we kill him in his two stages: stage 1, huge bone scythe; stage 2, horns become daggers. He dissolves as he dies.
  • Ahead we see an altar sucking power out of the top of the Spire.
  • Dagger is there masterminding all this and we confront him.
  • Try to talk him out of ruining the world again but all he wants to do is be alone in a cleansed Faeblheim.
  • We see him raising the Ceran Palace a hundred feet up on a stone platform, like another Worldspire.
  • He's too powerful but just as he's about to kill us Karl's crew show up. They help us. Boss loses an arm to the necrotic damage.
  • We hold him off long enough to crack the altar.

• S01: At front gates of city of Grima, capital of Ymlief, southern lands.
  • S01: Town of Grima has gone stale. No new players means no new resources.
  • S01: Head out to the distillery in Grima Darmstadt forest. Feta is the boss of the distillery. We obtain a bottle of whisky that turns people into frogs.

  • S05: Prepare to leave city of Grima.
  • S05: Situated between Grima and Tsarina palace.
  • S08: We journey by boat from Grima towards St Malia. Wrecked, washed up on an island. Pirates! We fuck them up.

  • S23: We journey southward towards Tsarina, and go to see Beurre the witch again. We tell her what we've learnt. She agrees to teleport us back to the Starter Tower above Grima. As soon as we arrive there with her, we are accosted by Karl and his cronies!
  • S24: We jump out of the Starter Tower. Something is wrong though. We can see Grima but also a hell dimension racing up towards us. We try to aim towards Grima.
  • S29: We had a long chat with L2 player character Aeschyl, who was in the tavern. Good guy. Convince him to head to Grima to try and achieve something useful.

docs.title: God Felling Star

The notes also raise the possibility that Faezel is connected to the God-Felling Star and may be the author of the recovered text.

I have this diary because Dikny insists I try Daily Pages, from a book she read back in the Old World. I think she used to be a playwright. No wonder she’s here.

docs.title: From Codex 1.0
docs.text:
• I have this diary because Dikny insists I try Daily Pages, from a book she read back in the Old World. I think she used to be a playwright. No wonder she’s here.
  • I honestly hate the thought of writing this shit down, but with everything that’s happening, she says it’ll help me decompress, and sort of my thoughts out. Let’s see. I guess I’m already starting. Ugh, Christ I hate this meta writing shit.
  • First thing’s first. My city is falling apart. Ever since those guys slipped through my filter, everything’s gone wrong. My home, once so comfortable and safe, is a terror town filled with people who don’t want to do anything for fear that they’ll upset someone else and get stabbed for it. Why did they tell everyone about pvp? What are they up to?
  • Boss suggests they were sent in by Hiki or Kimori – maybe even Monarchs? – to clear out the game. Remove the PVP block, then they come in and summon demons all over the place, all to kill as many people as possible so they can close it down without actively doing the killing themselves. But then why would Hiki or Kimori or anyone want to do that?
  • The alternative is they’re malicious people – or worse, fucking imbeciles - who stumbled into the key for demon summoning and are just doing whatever they want everywhere they go. Why? What joy is it bringing them to ruin other people’s bliss, and happiness?
  • Who do they think they are to police the fun other people are having? I don’t go to the community children’s paddling pool and knock their dicks outta their hands so why can’t they leave me the fuck alone in my game? It doesn’t matter what the queen does, exiling them, they’ll just raise hell wherever the fuck they are. Soon it’ll reach Grima.
  • Hell, it already is. Now I got people being recruited into the fucking army. Where is my city going? People killing each other, people being drafted. I got nothing left.
  • And they’ll have to pay.

docs.title: Megurash
docs.text:
Evil spirits from long ago, recognised by Emory after the party experienced visions of a staring face. They are threatening the giants again and appear connected to the murders across the Worldspire.

Known signs include ghostly figures that appear and vanish, psychic attacks, hallucinations, corpses with throat slashes and chest or back wounds, and spirits such as Midnakesh.

Their politics are tangled with the PvP ban, the morality of player death, and the question of whether Faeblheim should be preserved, reset or escaped.

docs.text:
Political and mechanical fault line around Faeblheim's strict ban on player-versus-player killing. The ban shapes the rise of the Player Killers, the moral conflict around Karl's crew, and the broader fear that someone might remove the safety rails to empty or reset the game.

First seen: Session 01; Last seen: Session 24.
